question_en: How often are the Summer Olympic Games held?
context_en: The Summer Olympics have been held every 4 years since 1896. The Winter Games too, alternating every 2 years with the Summer Games since 1994.

question_en: Which national team has won the most men's football World Cups?
context_en: Brazil has won 5 World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002). It is the only country to have played in every edition.

question_en: How many players does a basketball team have on the court?
context_en: Each team plays with 5 players. Basketball was invented by James Naismith in 1891 in Springfield, Massachusetts.

question_en: In which sport is a 'birdie' used?
context_en: In golf, a 'birdie' means one stroke under par for the hole. A 'bogey' is one over par and an 'eagle' two under.

question_en: How many players make up a football team on the field?
context_en: Each football team has 11 players, including the goalkeeper. The modern rules were codified in England in 1863.

question_en: In which country was modern tennis invented?
context_en: Modern tennis (lawn tennis) was invented by Walter Clopton Wingfield in England in 1873. Wimbledon, founded in 1877, is the oldest tournament.

question_en: What is the exact distance of a marathon?
context_en: A marathon is 42.195 km. The distance was set at the 1908 London Olympics based on the route from Windsor Castle to the stadium.

question_en: Who is the biggest Grand Slam winner in men's tennis?
context_en: Novak Djokovic leads with 24 Grand Slams (as of 2024). Nadal has 22 and Federer 20. Sampras stayed at 14.

question_en: What nationality was the cyclist Eddy Merckx?
context_en: Eddy Merckx is Belgian, winner of 5 Tours de France, 5 Giros and 1 Vuelta. He is considered the greatest cyclist in history.

question_en: In which sport is the Stanley Cup contested?
context_en: The Stanley Cup is the NHL trophy (ice hockey). It has been awarded since 1893; the Montreal Canadiens are the team with the most wins (24).

question_en: How many sets must a player win to win a best-of-5-sets match?
context_en: Men's Grand Slams are played to the best of 5 sets: you must win 3. Women's and most tournaments are played to the best of 3.

question_en: In which city were the first modern Olympics held in 1896?
context_en: The first modern Olympics were in Athens 1896, organized by Pierre de Coubertin. 241 athletes from 14 countries took part.

question_en: How many points is a 'try' worth in rugby union?
context_en: A try is worth 5 points in rugby union. The subsequent conversion adds 2 more points. A drop goal or penalty is worth 3.

question_en: In what year was the first football World Cup held?
context_en: The first World Cup was Uruguay 1930. The host nation, Uruguay, won it, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final.

question_en: What is the men's world record for the 100 meters?
context_en: Usain Bolt ran the 100 m in 9.58 s in Berlin 2009. It has been the world record ever since.

question_en: Which sport did Michael Phelps compete in?
context_en: Michael Phelps is the most decorated Olympic athlete in history: 28 medals (23 gold) in swimming, between 2004 and 2016.

question_en: How many holes does a standard golf course have?
context_en: A standard golf course has 18 holes. There are also 9-hole courses played twice to complete a round.

question_en: Which football club has won the most Champions Leagues?
context_en: Real Madrid leads with 15 Champions Leagues (as of 2024). They are followed by Milan (7) and Bayern and Liverpool (6).

question_en: In which sport did Diego Maradona excel?
context_en: Diego Armando Maradona (1960-2020) was an Argentine footballer, considered one of the best in history. World champion in 1986.

question_en: How many members does a volleyball team have on the court?
context_en: In volleyball, 6 per team play on the court. The sport was invented by William G. Morgan in 1895 in Massachusetts.

question_en: Who won the Tour de France 7 times in a row (later annulled)?
context_en: Lance Armstrong won 7 consecutive Tours (1999-2005), but they were stripped in 2012 for systematic doping with EPO.

question_en: Which team won the 2022 World Cup in Qatar?
context_en: Argentina won the 2022 World Cup, beating France on penalties after a 3-3 draw. It was Argentina's third world title.

question_en: What is the record for goals in a single European Championship?
context_en: Michel Platini (France) scored 9 goals at Euro 1984, an absolute record. Cristiano Ronaldo is the all-time top scorer with 14.

question_en: How many points is a three-pointer worth in basketball?
context_en: A shot from beyond the three-point line is worth 3. Inside it is worth 2 and a free throw is worth 1.

question_en: In what year did the Winter Olympics debut?
context_en: The first Winter Olympics were in Chamonix (France) in 1924. They were held in the same year as the Summer Games until 1992.

question_en: Which country invented judo?
context_en: Judo was created by Jigoro Kano in Japan in 1882, based on traditional jiu-jitsu. It was first Olympic at Tokyo 1964.

question_en: How many km do Formula 1 racers cover in a standard race?
context_en: An F1 race is ~305 km long (Monaco is the exception at 260). The maximum time limit is 2 hours of competition.

question_en: What color is the leader's jersey in the Vuelta a España?
context_en: The Vuelta leader has worn the red jersey since 2010. Before it was yellow, gold or other colors. In the Tour it is yellow and in the Giro pink.

question_en: In which sport is Pelé a legend?
context_en: Edson Arantes do Nascimento 'Pelé' (1940-2022) was a Brazilian footballer, the only one to win 3 World Cups (1958, 1962, 1970).

question_en: In what year did women's football debut at the Olympics?
context_en: Women's football debuted at Atlanta 1996, where the United States won gold. Men's football debuted at Paris 1900.

question_en: How many minutes does a regulation football match last?
context_en: A match lasts 90 minutes (2 halves of 45), with a 15-minute break. The referee adds time for stoppages.

question_en: In which country was badminton invented?
context_en: Modern badminton was developed at Badminton House (England) in 1873, although it derives from ancient racket games.

question_en: How many points does a home run add in baseball?
context_en: A home run is worth the points corresponding to the runners who reach home. With bases loaded, it is 4 points (a grand slam).

question_en: Which team won Euro 2020 (held in 2021)?
context_en: Italy won Euro 2020, beating England on penalties at Wembley. Roberto Mancini was the manager.

question_en: How much does an official football weigh?
context_en: An official football weighs between 410 and 450 grams at the start of the match, according to FIFA rules.

question_en: In which game do you win by achieving 'checkmate'?
context_en: In chess you win by delivering checkmate, attacking the enemy king with no escape.

question_en: Which female swimmer has won the most Olympic medals?
context_en: Jenny Thompson (USA) won 12 Olympic swimming medals between 1992 and 2004. Katie Ledecky has 10 as of 2024.

question_en: How many sets must a volleyball team win to take the match?
context_en: Volleyball is played to the best of 5 sets (to 25 points, the fifth to 15). You must win 3 sets.

question_en: In what year was the first Super Bowl held?
context_en: The first Super Bowl was in January 1967. The Green Bay Packers beat the Kansas City Chiefs 35-10.

question_en: What is Canada's national sport by law?
context_en: Canada declared by law in 1994 lacrosse as the national summer sport and ice hockey as the national winter sport.

question_en: How long is the Olympic athletics track?
context_en: A standard Olympic track measures 400 m in lane 1. The outer lanes are progressively longer.

question_en: In what year did Roger Bannister first break 4 minutes in the mile?
context_en: Roger Bannister was the first to run the mile under 4 minutes (3:59.4) on 6 May 1954 in Oxford.

question_en: How many gold medals did Mark Spitz win at Munich 1972?
context_en: Mark Spitz won 7 swimming golds at Munich 1972, a record only surpassed by Michael Phelps with 8 at Beijing 2008.

question_en: Which NBA player has the most rings?
context_en: Bill Russell won 11 rings with the Boston Celtics between 1957 and 1969. Michael Jordan won 6 with the Chicago Bulls.

question_en: Which sport is played at Wimbledon?
context_en: Wimbledon is the most prestigious tennis tournament, founded in 1877. It is played on grass and players are required to wear white.

question_en: How many points does a touchdown add in American football?
context_en: A touchdown is worth 6 points. Afterward you can convert 1 extra point (a kick) or 2 (a play from the 2-yard line).

question_en: Who is the all-time top scorer at the World Cup?
context_en: Germany's Miroslav Klose leads with 16 World Cup goals (2002-2014). Ronaldo Nazário scored 15 and Müller 14.

question_en: Which player is nicknamed 'La Pulga' (The Flea)?
context_en: Lionel Messi gets this nickname for his short stature and agility. He has won 8 Ballon d'Or awards, an all-time record.

question_en: In which sport do teams compete for the Webb Ellis Trophy?
context_en: The Webb Ellis Cup is the Rugby Union World Cup trophy, named after William Webb Ellis, the supposed inventor of the sport.

question_en: How many players does a water polo team have in play?
context_en: In water polo, 7 per team play (6 plus the goalkeeper). Matches are divided into 4 quarters of 8 minutes.

question_en: Who won the 2023 Ballon d'Or?
context_en: Lionel Messi won his 8th Ballon d'Or in 2023, after winning the Qatar 2022 World Cup with Argentina.

question_en: How many Ballon d'Or awards does Cristiano Ronaldo have?
context_en: Cristiano Ronaldo has won 5 Ballon d'Or awards (2008, 2013, 2014, 2016, 2017).

question_en: In what year was the first Women's Football World Cup held?
context_en: The first Women's Football World Cup was held in China in 1991. The United States won it.

question_en: Which team won the 2023 Women's Football World Cup?
context_en: Spain won the 2023 Women's World Cup held in Australia and New Zealand, beating England in the final (1-0).

question_en: In which sport did Tiger Woods excel?
context_en: Tiger Woods is one of the greatest golfers in history, with 15 majors won.

question_en: How many Grand Slams did Rafael Nadal win in his career?
context_en: Rafa Nadal won 22 Grand Slam titles, 14 of them at Roland Garros, where he is the undisputed king.

question_en: In which tournament is Nadal nicknamed the 'King of Clay'?
context_en: Rafa Nadal has won Roland Garros 14 times (2005-2022), an absolute record for a Grand Slam.

question_en: On which surface is Wimbledon played?
context_en: Wimbledon, the oldest Grand Slam (since 1877), is played on grass in London.

question_en: Who is the female tennis player with the most Grand Slams in the professional era?
context_en: Margaret Court has 24 Grand Slams (some in the amateur era). Serena Williams has 23 in the Open Era.

question_en: How many Grand Slams did Serena Williams win?
context_en: Serena Williams retired with 23 singles Grand Slam titles in the Open Era.

question_en: In what year did the Paralympic Games debut?
context_en: The first official Paralympic Games were held in Rome 1960, associated with the Olympics.

question_en: Which sport was invented in Canada in 1875?
context_en: The first organized ice hockey game was played in Montreal in 1875. It is Canada's national winter sport.

question_en: How many points is a free throw worth in basketball?
context_en: Each free throw made is worth 1 point. Field goals are worth 2 or 3 depending on the distance.

question_en: Which player scored 100 points in an NBA game?
context_en: Wilt Chamberlain scored 100 points for the Philadelphia Warriors on 2 March 1962. An all-time record.

question_en: Who is the all-time leading scorer in the NBA?
context_en: LeBron James surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar in February 2023 as the NBA's all-time leading scorer.

question_en: Which player won 6 NBA rings with the Chicago Bulls?
context_en: Michael Jordan won 6 NBA rings with the Bulls (1991, 1992, 1993, 1996, 1997, 1998) and was Finals MVP in all of them.

question_en: In what year was the NBA founded?
context_en: The NBA was founded as the Basketball Association of America (BAA) in 1946. It adopted its current name in 1949.

question_en: How many players does an American football team have on the field?
context_en: Each team has 11 players on the field, whether attacking or defending.

question_en: Which quarterback won 7 Super Bowls?
context_en: Tom Brady won 7 Super Bowls (6 with the New England Patriots and 1 with the Tampa Bay Buccaneers), an absolute record.

question_en: In which sport is a 'shuttlecock' used?
context_en: The shuttlecock is used in badminton. It can exceed 400 km/h in a smash.

question_en: How many sets are played in women's Grand Slam tennis?
context_en: Women's tennis is played to the best of 3 sets, also in Grand Slams. Men's is played to the best of 5.

question_en: In what year was VAR introduced in the Spanish league?
context_en: VAR (Video Assistant Referee) was implemented in LaLiga in the 2018-2019 season.

question_en: In what year did Spain win its only men's football World Cup?
context_en: Spain won the 2010 World Cup in South Africa, beating the Netherlands 1-0 in the final with a goal from Andrés Iniesta.

question_en: Who scored the decisive goal in the 2010 World Cup final?
context_en: Andrés Iniesta scored the goal in extra time (116th minute) in the final against the Netherlands.

question_en: Which team won Euro 2024?
context_en: Spain won Euro 2024 in Germany, their 4th, beating England 2-1 in the final.

question_en: In which sport did Magic Johnson excel?
context_en: Magic Johnson won 5 NBA rings with the Los Angeles Lakers in the 1980s. One of the legends of basketball.

question_en: Who is Usain Bolt?
context_en: Usain Bolt holds the world records for the 100 m (9.58 s) and 200 m (19.19 s). He won 8 Olympic golds.

question_en: In which game do you win by achieving 'checkmate'?
context_en: In chess, 'checkmate' means the king is threatened and has no possible escape.

question_en: Who is the current world chess champion (2024)?
context_en: D Gukesh, an 18-year-old Indian, became world champion by beating Ding Liren in December 2024.

question_en: Which Norwegian dominated world chess 2013-2023?
context_en: Magnus Carlsen was world champion from 2013 to 2023, when he gave up the title out of lack of interest.

question_en: Which sport is practiced in the Tour de France?
context_en: The Tour de France is the biggest stage cycling race in the world, held annually in July since 1903.

question_en: Who won the Tour de France 5 times (legally)?
context_en: Hinault, Merckx, Anquetil and Indurain each have 5 Tours. Armstrong was stripped for doping.

question_en: How many Tours in a row did Miguel Indurain win?
context_en: Miguel Indurain won 5 consecutive Tours de France (1991-1995), a unique feat in history.

question_en: Which Spanish driver won the F1 World Championship in 2005 and 2006?
context_en: Fernando Alonso won two consecutive world titles with Renault (2005 and 2006).

question_en: Who is the driver with the most F1 world titles?
context_en: Lewis Hamilton and Michael Schumacher share the record with 7 world titles each.

question_en: On which circuit is the Monaco Grand Prix held?
context_en: The Monaco Grand Prix is held on the Monte Carlo street circuit since 1929. It is one of the 'jewels' of F1.

question_en: Which Brazilian driver died at Imola in 1994?
context_en: Ayrton Senna died in a crash at the Tamburello corner of the Imola circuit on 1 May 1994.

question_en: Which sport does Marc Márquez practice?
context_en: Marc Márquez is a 6-time MotoGP world champion (2013-2014, 2016-2019). One of the great riders in history.

question_en: Who is Valentino Rossi?
context_en: Valentino Rossi won 9 motorcycling World Championships (1997-2009), 7 of them in the premier class.

question_en: How many km does an average stage of the Tour de France have?
context_en: The historical average is around 170-200 km per stage. The total Tour is usually about 3,500 km.

question_en: Which sport does Michael Phelps practice?
context_en: Michael Phelps is the most decorated Olympic athlete, with 28 medals (23 gold) in swimming.

question_en: In what year were the Barcelona Olympics held?
context_en: The Barcelona Olympic Games were held from 25 July to 9 August 1992.

question_en: In what year were the Tokyo 2020 Olympics held?
context_en: The Tokyo 2020 Olympics were postponed due to the pandemic and held in July-August 2021.

question_en: Which country will host the 2028 Summer Olympics?
context_en: The Los Angeles 2028 Olympics will be the third in the U.S. city (1932, 1984, 2028).

question_en: Which city hosted the 2024 Olympics?
context_en: Paris hosted the 2024 Olympics, 100 years after the 1924 Games. Opening ceremony along the Seine.

question_en: How many rings make up the Olympic symbol?
context_en: The five rings represent the five participating continents. They were used for the first time at the Antwerp 1920 Olympics.

question_en: Which color is missing from the Olympic rings (blue, yellow, black, green, ...)?
context_en: The five colors are blue, yellow, black, green and red, on a white background.

question_en: How many players make up a handball team on the court?
context_en: Each handball team plays with 7 players (6 field players + goalkeeper).

question_en: In which sport is Nikolai Valuev a legend?
context_en: Nikolai Valuev, a 2.13 m Russian, was WBA heavyweight boxing world champion (2005-2009).

question_en: Who is Muhammad Ali?
context_en: Muhammad Ali (1942-2016) is considered one of the greatest boxers of all time. Three-time world champion.

question_en: Which phrase is famous from Muhammad Ali?
options_en: Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee
context_en: His signature phrase was 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee'.

question_en: In which sport is the America's Cup contested?
context_en: The 'Copa América' is both the South American national football tournament and (America's Cup) the oldest sailing regatta in the world.

question_en: In which sport did Pau Gasol excel?
context_en: Pau Gasol won 2 NBA rings with the Los Angeles Lakers (2009, 2010) and a World Cup with Spain (2006).

question_en: Who is Severiano Ballesteros?
context_en: Seve Ballesteros (1957-2011) won 5 golf majors and was a pioneer of European golf. Three British Opens and two Masters.

question_en: Which national team dominated world rugby with its 'haka'?
context_en: The All Blacks of New Zealand perform the Maori haka before each match. They have won the World Cup 3 times.

question_en: How many players play rugby union on the field per team?
context_en: Each rugby union team plays with 15 players. Rugby league (a variant) is played with 13.

question_en: Which sport was invented in England in the 18th century?
context_en: Cricket has been documented in England since the 16th century. It is the national sport of India and other Commonwealth countries.

question_en: In which sport did Sachin Tendulkar excel?
context_en: Sachin Tendulkar, 'the little master', is probably the greatest cricket player in history. Indian.

question_en: In which sport did Hicham El Guerrouj excel?
context_en: The Moroccan Hicham El Guerrouj holds the world records for the 1,500 m and the mile. Double gold at Athens 2004.

question_en: Who broke the marathon world record in 2023?
context_en: Kelvin Kiptum broke the world record at Chicago 2023 with 2h00:35. He died tragically in 2024.

question_en: What is the women's world record for the marathon?
context_en: Ruth Chepngetich (Kenya) ran the Chicago marathon 2024 in 2h09:56, the first woman under 2h10.

question_en: In what year was the 3-point line introduced in the NBA?
context_en: The 3-point line was introduced in the 1979-1980 season. FIBA adopted it in 1984.

question_en: In which sport do you win by being the first to cross the finish line?
context_en: In athletics (races), the first to cross the finish line wins. There are distances from 100 m to the marathon.

question_en: In which sport is the word 'ace' used?
context_en: In tennis, an 'ace' is a winning serve that the receiver does not even touch with the racket.

question_en: In which sport is the term 'knockout' (KO) used?
context_en: In boxing, a knockout is when the opponent cannot get up before the referee's count of 10.

question_en: In which sport is the 'Webb Ellis Trophy' awarded?
context_en: The Webb Ellis Trophy is awarded to the winner of the Rugby World Cup. William Webb Ellis 'invented' rugby in 1823.

question_en: In what year was the first Rugby World Cup held?
context_en: The first Rugby World Cup was organized in Australia and New Zealand in 1987. New Zealand won it.

question_en: In which sport did Pelé excel?
context_en: Pelé (Edson Arantes do Nascimento, 1940-2022) won 3 World Cups with Brazil (1958, 1962, 1970), a unique record.

question_en: How many World Cups did Lionel Messi play?
context_en: Messi played 5 World Cups with Argentina (2006, 2010, 2014, 2018, 2022). He won the 2022 one in Qatar.

question_en: In what year was FIFA founded?
context_en: FIFA was founded on 21 May 1904 in Paris with 7 national federations.

question_en: Who is the all-time top scorer for Real Madrid?
context_en: Cristiano Ronaldo is Real Madrid's all-time top scorer with 450 goals in 438 matches (2009-2018).

question_en: In what year was FC Barcelona founded?
context_en: FC Barcelona was founded on 29 November 1899 by Joan Gamper.

question_en: In what year was Real Madrid founded?
context_en: Real Madrid was founded on 6 March 1902 as Madrid Football Club.

question_en: Who is the all-time top scorer for FC Barcelona?
context_en: Lionel Messi scored 672 goals with FC Barcelona (2004-2021), an absolute club record.

question_en: In which sport did Cassius Clay excel?
context_en: Cassius Clay is the birth name of Muhammad Ali. He changed his name in 1964 when he converted to Islam.

question_en: Which team won the 2024 Copa América?
context_en: Argentina won the 2024 Copa América in the U.S., beating Colombia 1-0 in the final.

question_en: In what year did Athletic Bilbao win the Copa del Rey last (before 2024)?
context_en: Athletic won the 1984 Cup before winning the 2024 one, ending a 40-year drought.

question_en: How many minutes does an NBA quarter last?
context_en: Each NBA quarter lasts 12 minutes. A total NBA game: 48 minutes. In FIBA it is 10 min/quarter (40 min total).

question_en: How many players are on a padel team?
context_en: Padel is always played in pairs (2 vs 2). It is a sport born in Mexico in 1969.

question_en: In which country did padel become popular?
context_en: Padel was born in Mexico (1969), spread through Argentina and became established in Spain, where it is very popular.

question_en: In which sport did the Williams sisters excel?
context_en: Venus and Serena Williams dominated world women's tennis for more than two decades.

question_en: In what year did Lionel Messi debut with FC Barcelona?
context_en: Messi debuted with FC Barcelona on 16 October 2004 against Espanyol.

question_en: How many goals did Pelé officially score in his career?
context_en: Pelé scored 757 goals in official matches according to FIFA, although he claimed 1,281 including friendlies.

question_en: In which sport do you win by achieving a 'hat-trick'?
context_en: In football and hockey, a hat-trick is scoring 3 goals in a single match. The term comes from cricket.

question_en: How many players does a baseball team have in play?
context_en: A baseball team fields 9 players (including the pitcher and the catcher).

question_en: Which sport was invented in the United States in 1891?
context_en: James Naismith invented basketball in Springfield (Massachusetts) in December 1891.

question_en: In what year was volleyball invented?
context_en: William G. Morgan invented volleyball in 1895 in Holyoke (Massachusetts), originally called 'mintonette'.

question_en: How many players are on a beach volleyball team?
context_en: Beach volleyball is played 2 vs 2. It has been an Olympic sport since Atlanta 1996.

question_en: In which sport is the Ryder Cup contested?
context_en: The Ryder Cup is the team golf competition between Europe and the U.S., held every 2 years since 1927.

question_en: Who is Lewis Hamilton?
context_en: Lewis Hamilton is a 7-time F1 world champion. The driver with the most wins and poles in history.

question_en: Which sport is played with a flying disc between teams?
context_en: Ultimate frisbee is played with a disc between two teams on a field similar to an American football field.

question_en: Which American swimmer won 7 golds at Tokyo 2020/2021?
context_en: Katie Ledecky is one of the great swimmers in history, with multiple Olympic golds. (Caeleb Dressel also won multiple in Tokyo.)

question_en: In what year did football debut as an Olympic sport?
context_en: Men's football debuted as an Olympic sport at Paris 1900 (a demonstration) and became official at London 1908.

question_en: In which sport is the 'Maillot Jaune' contested?
context_en: The yellow jersey (maillot jaune) is worn by the leader of the general classification of the Tour de France.

question_en: What color is the leader's jersey in the Giro d'Italia?
context_en: The pink jersey (maglia rosa) has been that of the Giro d'Italia leader since 1931. The color honors the newspaper La Gazzetta dello Sport.

question_en: In which sport did Roger Federer excel?
context_en: Roger Federer won 20 Grand Slams. Considered one of the greatest tennis players of all time.

question_en: How many points must a team reach to win a volleyball set?
context_en: Each volleyball set is won by reaching 25 points with a 2-point lead (except the 5th set, to 15).

question_en: In which sport did Nadia Comăneci excel?
context_en: Nadia Comăneci (Romania) was the first gymnast to score a perfect 10, at the Montreal 1976 Olympics.

question_en: Which American football player was nicknamed 'Sweetness'?
context_en: Walter Payton, a Chicago Bears legend, was nicknamed 'Sweetness' for his elegance when running.

question_en: In which sport is the 'Heisman Trophy' contested?
context_en: The Heisman Trophy has been awarded to the best college American football player since 1935.
